page: Amazon Web Services m7g.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Graviton3 processor family, utilizing the arm64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores (64 vCPUs) based on the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core, and a shared 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

In benchmark testing, the m7g.16xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core performance and memory subsystem efficiency. Its multi-core stress-ng score of 97,878.33 and Geekbench multi-core score of 20,663.0 place it in the strong and top-tier performance brackets, respectively. The memory architecture delivers a top-tier PassMark memory score, with read-only large block memory bandwidth reaching 253.34 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 121.49 GB/sec. Software compilation via Clang also registers in the top tier. However, single-core performance remains in the average range, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1,523.0.

The server provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. It lacks local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes, which represents a tradeoff for I/O-heavy local database workloads. Despite this, database operations perform strongly, with Redis SET operations achieving 101.03 million operations per second. The instance is highly cost-efficient for scale-out workloads due to its dedicated physical cores and high resource density. It is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, software compilation, multi-threaded data processing, and medium-sized language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.16xlarge is an arm64-based general-purpose server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton3 (Neoverse-V1) processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, alongside strong database and software compilation capabilities. Single-core performance and text generation for very small LLMs are average to poor, highlighting a tradeoff favoring parallelized, multi-threaded workloads. It is highly suited for static web serving, software compilation, and distributed database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.16xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.16xlarge is a 64-core arm64 general-purpose server with 256 GB RAM, running on the Nitro hypervisor with 30 Gbps bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core arm64 server delivering high memory bandwidth and strong multi-threaded performance for balanced general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 cores running at 2.6 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
